mission.title: Install and Configure Inspektor Gadget on Kubernetes
mission.description: Inspektor Gadget is a set of tools and framework for data collection and system inspection on Kubernetes clusters and Linux hosts using eBPF. Installing it allows you to leverage eBPF for enhanced observability and monitoring of your applications and infrastructure.

steps.title: Add the Inspektor Gadget Helm repository
steps.description:
Add the official Helm repository for Inspektor Gadget to your Helm configuration.
```bash
helm repo add inspektor-gadget https://inspektor-gadget.github.io/charts
helm repo update
```

steps.title: Install Inspektor Gadget using Helm
steps.description:
Install Inspektor Gadget in the `gadget` namespace (the official default). This command will also create the namespace if it doesn't exist.
```bash
helm install inspektor-gadget inspektor-gadget/gadget --namespace gadget --create-namespace --version v0.49.1
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check that the Inspektor Gadget pods are running successfully in the `gadget` namespace. Note that Inspektor Gadget runs as a DaemonSet, so you should see one pod per node.
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace gadget
```

steps.title: Use the kubectl-gadget CLI
steps.description:
Inspektor Gadget is a CLI-based tool — it does not provide a web UI or dashboard. Install the `kubectl-gadget` plugin to interact with it:
```bash
kubectl gadget version
kubectl gadget top ebpf --namespace gadget
```

resolution.summary: A successful installation will show the Inspektor Gadget pods in the `Running` state when you run `kubectl get pods --namespace gadget`. Inspektor Gadget is a CLI tool — use `kubectl gadget` commands to inspect your cluster.
codeSnippets:
helm repo add inspektor-gadget https://inspektor-gadget.github.io/charts
helm repo update
helm install inspektor-gadget inspektor-gadget/gadget --namespace gadget --create-namespace --version v0.49.1

uninstall.title: Remove the Helm release
uninstall.description:
Uninstall the Inspektor Gadget release from the cluster using Helm. This will remove the DaemonSet and associated resources created by the installation.
```bash
helm uninstall inspektor-gadget --namespace gadget
```

uninstall.title: Clean up CRDs and persistent resources
uninstall.description:
Remove any Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) and persistent volume claims (PVCs) that were created during the installation. This ensures that all resources related to Inspektor Gadget are cleaned up.
```bash
kubectl delete crd gadgets.inspektor-gadget.io
kubectl delete pvc --all --namespace gadget
```

uninstall.title: Remove namespace and verify
uninstall.description:
Delete the `gadget` namespace to remove any remaining resources. Verify that the namespace has been deleted successfully.
```bash
kubectl delete namespace gadget
kubectl get namespaces
```

upgrade.title: Backup current state
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, it's a good practice to back up the current state of your Inspektor Gadget installation. You can do this by exporting the current configuration.
```bash
kubectl get all --namespace gadget -o yaml > inspektor-gadget-backup.yaml
```

upgrade.title: Update repo and run upgrade command
upgrade.description:
Update the Helm repository and upgrade the Inspektor Gadget release to the latest version. Make sure to specify the desired version if needed.
```bash
helm repo update
helm upgrade inspektor-gadget inspektor-gadget/gadget --namespace gadget --version v0.49.2
```

upgrade.title: Verify the upgrade
upgrade.description:
Check that the Inspektor Gadget pods are running the new version successfully after the upgrade.
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace gadget
```

troubleshooting.title: Pods stuck in CrashLoopBackOff
troubleshooting.description:
If the Inspektor Gadget pods are in a CrashLoopBackOff state, check the logs for the pods to diagnose the issue.
```bash
kubectl logs <pod-name> --namespace gadget
```

troubleshooting.title: kubectl gadget plugin not found
troubleshooting.description:
If the `kubectl gadget` command is not found, install the kubectl-gadget plugin. See the official documentation for installation instructions:
```bash
kubectl krew install gadget
```

troubleshooting.title: Insufficient resources
troubleshooting.description:
If the pods are not starting due to insufficient resources, check the resource requests and limits defined in the DaemonSet. You may need to adjust them or allocate more resources to your cluster.
```bash
kubectl describe daemonset inspektor-gadget --namespace gadget
```

troubleshooting.title: CRDs not found
troubleshooting.description:
If you encounter issues related to CRDs, ensure that the CRDs were installed correctly. You can list the CRDs to verify their presence.
```bash
kubectl get crd | grep gadgets
```

prerequisites.description: You need a Kubernetes cluster running version 1.24 or higher, along with Helm and kubectl installed on your local machine.
